heading: Single-employer plan benefits guaranteed
label: 1322 Single-employer plan benefits guaranteed
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(3), is , . Titles II, and XVI of the Act are classified generally to subchapters II (§ 401 et seq.) and XVI (§ 1381 et seq.), respectively, of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Amendments2006—Subsec. (b)(5). , amended par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (5) related to the amount of benefits guaranteed under this section in the case of a participant in a plan who was covered by the plan as a substantial owner.Pub. L. 109–280, § 407(a)Subsec. (b)(8). , added par. (8).Pub. L. 109–280, § 403(a)Subsec. (c)(3)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “Except as provided in subparagraph (C), for purposes of this subsection, the term ‘recovery ratio’ means the average ratio, with respect to prior plan terminations described in subparagraph (B), of—Pub. L. 109–280, § 408(b)(1)“(i) the value of the recovery of the corporation under section 1362, 1363, or 1364 of this title in connection with such prior terminations, to“(ii) the amount of unfunded benefit liabilities under such plans as of the termination date in connection with such prior terminations.”Subsec. (c)(3)(B)(ii). , amended cl. (ii) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (ii) read as follows: “notices of intent to terminate were provided after , and during the 5-Federal fiscal year period ending with the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year in which occurs the date of the notice of intent to terminate with respect to the plan termination for which the recovery ratio is being determined.”Pub. L. 109–280, § 408(a)December 17, 1987Subsec. (g). , which directed amendment of this section, as amended by , by adding subsec. (g) at the end, was executed by adding subsec. (g) after subsec. (f) and before subsec. (h) to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 109–280, § 404(a)Pub. L. 109–280Subsec. (h). , added subsec. (h).Pub. L. 109–280, § 402(g)(2)(A)1994—Subsec. (b)(3). , inserted at end “The maximum guaranteed monthly benefit shall not be reduced solely on account of the age of a participant in the case of a benefit payable by reason of disability that occurred on or before the termination date, if the participant demonstrates to the satisfaction of the corporation that the Social Security Administration has determined that the participant satisfies the definition of disability under title II or XVI of the Social Security Act, and the regulations thereunder. If a benefit payable by reason of disability is converted to an early or normal retirement benefit for reasons other than a change in the health of the participant, such early or normal retirement benefit shall be treated as a continuation of the benefit payable by reason of disability and this subparagraph shall continue to apply.”Pub. children.heading: Nonforfeitable benefits
children.label: a Nonforfeitable benefits
children.text: Subject to the limitations contained in subsection (b), the corporation shall guarantee, in accordance with this section, the payment of all nonforfeitable benefits (other than benefits becoming nonforfeitable solely on account of the termination of a plan) under a single-employer plan which terminates at a time when this subchapter applies to it.

children.label: A
children.text: no benefits provided by a plan which has been in effect for less than 60 months at the time the plan terminates shall be guaranteed under this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: any increase in the amount of benefits under a plan resulting from a plan amendment which was made, or became effective, whichever is later, within 60 months before the date on which the plan terminates shall be disregarded.

For purposes of this subsection, the time a successor plan (within the meaning of ) has been in effect includes the time a previously established plan (within the meaning of ) was in effect. For purposes of determining what benefits are guaranteed under this section in the case of a plan to which does not apply on , the 60-month period referred to in paragraph (1) shall be computed beginning on the first date on which such section does apply to the plan.section 1321(a) of this titlesection 1321(a) of this titlesection 1321 of this titleSeptember 3, 1974

children.label: A
children.text: his average monthly gross income from his employer during the 5 consecutive calendar year period (or, if less, during the number of calendar years in such period in which he actively participates in the plan) during which his gross income from that employer was greater than during any other such period with that employer determined by dividing ⁄ of the sum of all such gross income by the number of such calendar years in which he had such gross income, or112

children.label: B
children.text: $750 multiplied by a fraction, the numerator of which is the contribution and benefit base (determined under section 230 of the Social Security Act []) in effect at the time the plan terminates and the denominator of which is such contribution and benefit base in effect in calendar year 1974.42 U.S.C. 430

children.label: A
children.text: The actuarial value of a benefit, for purposes of this subsection, shall be determined in accordance with regulations prescribed by the corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the term “gross income” means “earned income” within the meaning of (determined without regard to any community property laws),section 911(b) of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a participant in a plan under which contributions are made by more than one employer, amounts received as gross income from any employer under that plan shall be aggregated with amounts received from any other employer under that plan during the same period, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any non-basic benefit shall be disregarded.

children.label: i
children.text: owns the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a partnership, is a partner who owns, directly or indirectly, 50 percent or more of either the capital interest or the profits interest in such partnership, or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a corporation, owns, directly or indirectly, 50 percent or more in value of either the voting stock of that corporation or all the stock of that corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: a fraction (not to exceed 1) the numerator of which is the number of years from the later of the effective date or the adoption date of the plan to the termination date, and the denominator of which is 10, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of benefits that would be guaranteed under this section if the participant were not a majority owner.

children.label: A
children.text: No benefits accrued under a plan after the date on which the Secretary of the Treasury issues notice that he has determined that any trust which is a part of a plan does not meet the requirements of , or that the plan does not meet the requirements of , are guaranteed under this section unless such determination is erroneous. This subparagraph does not apply if the Secretary subsequently issues a notice that such trust meets the requirements of or that the plan meets the requirements of and if the Secretary determines that the trust or plan has taken action necessary to meet such requirements during the period between the issuance of the notice referred to in the preceding sentence and the issuance of the notice referred to in this sentence.section 401(a) of title 26section 404(a)(2) of title 26section 401(a) of title 26section 404(a)(2) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: No benefits accrued under a plan after the date on which an amendment of the plan is adopted which causes the Secretary of the Treasury to determine that any trust under the plan has ceased to meet the requirements of or that the plan has ceased to meet the requirements of , are guaranteed under this section unless such determination is erroneous. This subparagraph shall not apply if the amendment is revoked as of the date it was first effective or amended to comply with such requirements.section 401(a) of title 26section 404(a)(2) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: 20 percent of the amount which, but for the fact that the plan or amendment has not been in effect for 60 months or more, would be guaranteed under this section, or

children.label: B
children.text: $20 per month,

If an unpredictable contingent event benefit (as defined in ) is payable by reason of the occurrence of any event, this section shall be applied as if a plan amendment had been adopted on the date such event occurred.section 1056(g)(1) of this title

children.heading: Payment by corporation to participants and beneficiaries of recovery percentage of outstanding amount of benefit liabilities
children.label: c Payment by corporation to participants and beneficiaries of recovery percentage of outstanding amount of benefit liabilities

children.text: In addition to benefits paid under the preceding provisions of this section with respect to a terminated plan, the corporation shall pay the portion of the amount determined under paragraph (2) which is allocated with respect to each participant under . Such payment shall be made to such participant or to such participant’s beneficiaries (including alternate payees, within the meaning of ).section 1344(a) of this titlesection 1056(d)(3)(K) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the outstanding amount of benefit liabilities under the plan (including interest calculated from the termination date), by

children.label: B
children.text: the applicable recovery ratio.

children.label: i
children.text: the sum of the values of all recoveries under section 1362, 1363, or 1364 of this title, determined by the corporation in connection with plan terminations described under subparagraph (B), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the sum of all unfunded benefit liabilities under such plans as of the termination date in connection with any such prior termination.

children.label: i
children.text: the corporation has determined the value of recoveries under section 1362, 1363, or 1364 of this title, and

children.label: ii
children.text: notices of intent to terminate were provided (or in the case of a termination by the corporation, a notice of determination under was issued) during the 5-Federal fiscal year period ending with the third fiscal year preceding the fiscal year in which occurs the date of the notice of intent to terminate (or the notice of determination under ) with respect to the plan termination for which the recovery ratio is being determined.section 1342 of this titlesection 1342 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the value of the recoveries of the corporation under section 1362, 1363, or 1364 of this title in connection with such plan, to

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of unfunded benefit liabilities under such plan as of the termination date.
Determinations under this subsection shall be made by the corporation. Such determinations shall be binding unless shown by clear and convincing evidence to be unreasonable.

children.heading: Authorization to guarantee other classes of benefits
children.label: d Authorization to guarantee other classes of benefits
children.text: The corporation is authorized to guarantee the payment of such other classes of benefits and to establish the terms and conditions under which such other classes of benefits are guaranteed as it determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Nonforfeitability of preretirement survivor annuity
children.label: e Nonforfeitability of preretirement survivor annuity
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), a qualified preretirement survivor annuity (as defined in ) with respect to a participant under a terminated single-employer plan shall not be treated as forfeitable solely because the participant has not died as of the termination date.section 1055(e)(1) of this title

children.heading: Effective date of plan amendments
children.label: f Effective date of plan amendments
children.text: For purposes of this section, the effective date of a plan amendment described in shall be the effective date of the plan of reorganization of the employer described in or, if later, the effective date stated in such amendment.section 1054(i)(1) of this titlesection 1054(i)(1) of this title

children.heading: Bankruptcy filing substituted for termination date
children.label: g Bankruptcy filing substituted for termination date
children.text: If a contributing sponsor of a plan has filed or has had filed against such person a petition seeking liquidation or reorganization in a case under title 11 or under any similar Federal law or law of a State or political subdivision, and the case has not been dismissed as of the termination date of the plan, then this section shall be applied by treating the date such petition was filed as the termination date of the plan.

children.heading: Special rule for plans electing certain funding requirements
children.label: h Special rule for plans electing certain funding requirements
A
children.text: by treating the first day of the first applicable plan year as the termination date of the plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: by determining the amount of guaranteed benefits on the basis of plan assets and liabilities as of such assumed termination date, and

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of guaranteed benefits under this section (determined without regard to paragraph (1) and on the basis of plan assets and liabilities as of the actual date of plan termination), exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the amount determined under paragraph (1).
